name: Weblate
what_it_does: Open-source web-based translation management system with a full REST API for managing projects, components, translation strings, glossaries, and screenshots — storing translations directly in your Git repository.
use_cases: Automating translation pipeline orchestration: triggering translation jobs, monitoring completion, and merging translated strings via API
Agents syncing localization status across projects — querying untranslated string counts, completion percentages, and quality checks
Programmatically managing translation memory and glossaries to maintain terminology consistency across products
CI/CD integration: auto-committing new source strings to Weblate on code push and auto-merging completed translations back to the repo
not_for: Teams who need a fully managed translation service with professional human translator networks (use Phrase or Lokalise for that)
Organizations that cannot self-host and need enterprise SLAs without managing infrastructure
Simple single-language apps with static content that doesn't change frequently
best_when: Best when a development team wants Git-native continuous localization with full API control, is comfortable self-hosting, and wants translators working in a polished web UI while developers interact programmatically via REST.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need a managed platform with built-in professional translation ordering, or when your team has no capacity to maintain a self-hosted Weblate instance (Docker or manual deployment).

auth.notes: API token per user, passed as 'Token <key>' in the Authorization header. Tokens carry the same permissions as the user account — no fine-grained scope support. Python CLI client (wlc) uses the same token mechanism. Weblate Hosted and self-hosted instances both use this approach.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted (GPLv3) is fully free with no feature limitations. Weblate Hosted libre tier available at no cost for open-source projects.
pricing.paid_tiers: Weblate Hosted plans start at €23/month for small teams. Business and Enterprise plans scale by user count and feature set. Dedicated hosted instances available for larger organizations.
pricing.notes: Self-hosted on your own infrastructure has no software costs. Weblate Hosted is the SaaS version run by the Weblate team. Open-source projects can apply for the gratis libre tier on Hosted.

performance.rate_limits: REST API rate limits are configurable per deployment. Weblate Hosted applies fair-use rate limiting but does not publicly specify exact request-per-minute limits. Self-hosted instances have no platform-imposed limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Weblate REST API returns well-structured JSON error responses with field-level validation details. HTTP 400 errors include a list of specific field errors. Permission errors (403) are clear. The API is DRF-based (Django REST Framework) with consistent error formatting.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Translation string updates are naturally idempotent (PUT/PATCH by key). Component and project creation via POST is not idempotent — check for existence before creating to avoid conflicts.
known_agent_gotchas: API slugs for projects and components must be known; there is no fuzzy name lookup — agents must fetch the full list to find identifiers
Git repository operations (commit, push, pull) are triggered via separate API actions, not automatic — agents must explicitly trigger commits after translation updates
Locking a component for translation (to prevent conflicts during bulk API updates) must be explicitly managed and unlocked when done
The wlc Python CLI tool is a wrapper around the REST API and does not add any capabilities beyond what the REST API provides directly
Self-hosted Weblate version may lag behind Hosted in API features — verify API version compatibility when writing automation against self-hosted deployments
Translation memory suggestions and machine translation integrations (DeepL, LibreTranslate) are UI-driven; triggering them programmatically requires separate API calls

security_score_components.security_notes: User-scoped API tokens with no permission granularity — a compromised token grants full user access including repository operations. Self-hosted operators must configure TLS and network access controls. Open-source codebase (GPLv3) is fully auditable. GDPR-compliant data handling on Hosted.
